input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule EV.5.5 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.5.5.3
            TITLE: a dummy connector or similar may be used to restore isolation to meet ev.6.1.2

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.5.3: A dummy connector or similar may be used to restore isolation to meet EV.6.1.2
EV.5.5.3 A dummy connector or similar may be used to restore isolation to meet EV.6.1.2

RULE: ev.5.5.2
            TITLE: an interlock ev.7.8 must open the shutdown circuit ev.7.2.2 when the hvd is removed

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.5.2: An Interlock EV.7.8 must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 when the HVD is removed
EV.5.5.2 An Interlock EV.7.8 must Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 when the HVD is removed

RULE: ev.5.5
            TITLE: high voltage disconnect - hvd

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.5: High Voltage Disconnect - HVD
EV.5.5 High Voltage Disconnect - HVD 
A High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be included to quickly disconnect one or both poles of 
the Accumulator  EV.11.3.2

RULE: ev.5.5.1
            TITLE: the high voltage disconnect (hvd) must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.5.1: The High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be:
EV.5.5.1 The High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be: 
a. A directly accessible element, fuse or connector  
b. More than 350 mm from the ground 
c. Easily visible when standing behind the vehicle  
d. Operable in 10 seconds or less by an untrained person 
e. Operable without removing any bodywork or obstruction or using tools  
f. Directly operated.  Remote operation through a long handle, rope or wire is not 
acceptable.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 95 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
g. Clearly marked with "HVD"

final_answer: rule EV.5.5: High Voltage Disconnect - HVD A High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be included to quickly disconnect one or both poles of the Accumulator EV.11.3.2
